testCases.title: Primary action buttons should be high-contrast and optimized for thumb-reachability on mobile platforms
preconditions: User is on the login/register screen on a mobile device

steps.content: Observe the primary action button (e.g., 'Login' or 'Sign Up')
steps.expected: The primary action button has high contrast against the background, making it easily visible.

steps.content: Attempt to interact with the primary action button using a thumb
steps.expected: The button is positioned and sized to be easily reachable and tappable with a thumb, indicating optimization for mobile users.

testCases.expectedResult: Primary action buttons are high-contrast and optimized for thumb-reachability on mobile platforms.

testCases.title: User should be able to sign in successfully when valid credentials are provided
preconditions: User is on the 'Sign In' screen
A registered user account exists with known valid credentials (e.g., email: 'test@example.com', password: 'Password123!')

steps.content: Enter 'test@example.com' into the email input field
steps.expected: The email field accepts the input.

steps.content: Enter 'Password123!' into the password input field
steps.expected: The password field accepts the input and masks the characters.

steps.content: Click the 'Login' primary action button
steps.expected: The application processes the login request. User is redirected to the authenticated home screen or dashboard.

testCases.expectedResult: User successfully logs in and is navigated to the authenticated section of the application.

testCases.title: Login should fail and an error message should display when user provides an incorrect password
preconditions: User is on the 'Sign In' screen
A registered user account exists with known valid email (e.g., email: 'test@example.com')

steps.content: Enter 'test@example.com' into the email input field
steps.expected: The email field accepts the input.

steps.content: Enter an incorrect password (e.g., 'WrongPass123!') into the password input field
steps.expected: The password field accepts the input and masks the characters.

steps.content: Click the 'Login' primary action button
steps.expected: Login fails. An error message indicating incorrect credentials (e.g., 'Incorrect email or password') is displayed on the screen. The 'Forgot Password' link is visible.

testCases.expectedResult: Login fails with an appropriate error message, and a direct path to 'Forgot Password' is available.

testCases.title: Login should fail and an error message should display when user provides an unregistered email address
preconditions: User is on the 'Sign In' screen

steps.content: Enter an unregistered email address (e.g., 'nonexistent@example.com') into the email input field
steps.expected: The email field accepts the input.

steps.content: Enter a valid-format password (e.g., 'Password123!') into the password input field
steps.expected: The password field accepts the input and masks the characters.

steps.content: Click the 'Login' primary action button
steps.expected: Login fails. An error message indicating incorrect credentials (e.g., 'Incorrect email or password') is displayed on the screen. The 'Forgot Password' link is visible.

testCases.expectedResult: Login fails with an appropriate error message for an unregistered email, and a direct path to 'Forgot Password' is available.

testCases.title: Forgot Password link should be accessible after any failed login attempt
preconditions: User is on the 'Sign In' screen

steps.content: Attempt to log in with invalid credentials (e.g., 'invalid@example.com', 'wrong')
steps.expected: Login fails, and an error message is displayed.

steps.content: Locate the 'Forgot Password' link/button
steps.expected: A clearly visible 'Forgot Password' link or button is present on the screen.

steps.content: Click the 'Forgot Password' link/button
steps.expected: User is navigated to the 'Forgot Password' flow/page.

testCases.expectedResult: The 'Forgot Password' flow is directly accessible from any failed login attempt.

testCases.title: User should be able to register successfully when unique and valid details are provided
preconditions: User is on the 'Sign Up' screen

steps.content: Enter a unique, valid email address (e.g., 'newuser@example.com') into the email input field
steps.expected: The email field accepts the input without inline errors.

steps.content: Enter a strong password meeting all requirements (e.g., 'NewPass!123') into the password input field
steps.expected: The password field accepts the input, masks characters, and inline validation feedback indicates a strong password. Contextual assistance for password requirements disappears.

steps.content: Click the 'Sign Up' primary action button
steps.expected: The application processes the registration request. User is successfully registered and logged in, then redirected to the authenticated home screen or dashboard.

testCases.expectedResult: User successfully registers and is navigated to the authenticated section of the application.

testCases.title: Registration should fail and an error message should display when user attempts to register with an already existing email
preconditions: User is on the 'Sign Up' screen
A registered user account exists with known email (e.g., email: 'existing@example.com')

steps.content: Enter 'existing@example.com' into the email input field
steps.expected: The email field accepts the input.

steps.content: Enter a valid password (e.g., 'Password123!') into the password input field
steps.expected: The password field accepts the input and masks characters.

steps.content: Click the 'Sign Up' primary action button
steps.expected: Registration fails. An error message indicating the email is already in use (e.g., 'This email is already registered') is displayed.

testCases.expectedResult: Registration fails with an error message indicating the email is already in use.

testCases.title: Registration should fail and inline validation should display when password does not meet strength requirements
preconditions: User is on the 'Sign Up' screen

steps.content: Enter a unique, valid email address (e.g., 'testregister@example.com') into the email input field
steps.expected: The email field accepts the input without inline errors.

steps.content: Enter a weak password (e.g., 'short') into the password input field
steps.expected: The password field accepts the input. Inline validation feedback (e.g., 'Password too short', 'Needs uppercase letter') is displayed directly beneath the input field. Contextual assistance for password requirements remains visible.

steps.content: Click the 'Sign Up' primary action button
steps.expected: The 'Sign Up' button remains disabled or registration fails with an error message, and the inline validation messages persist.

testCases.expectedResult: Registration fails due to weak password, with real-time inline validation feedback and contextual assistance.

testCases.title: for mobile-web only
preconditions: testing

steps.content: testing
steps.expected: testing

testCases.title: Email input field should display instant inline feedback for invalid email format
preconditions: User is on the 'Sign In' or 'Sign Up' screen

steps.content: Enter 'invalid-email' (without '@' symbol) into the email input field
steps.expected: The email field accepts the input. Instant, inline feedback (e.g., 'Invalid email format' or 'Please enter a valid email address') is displayed directly beneath the email field.

steps.content: Continue typing to form a valid email (e.g., 'invalid-email@domain.com')
steps.expected: The inline validation feedback for invalid format disappears as the email becomes valid.

testCases.expectedResult: Email field provides instant, inline validation feedback for format errors that disappears upon correction.

testCases.title: Password input field should display real-time inline feedback for password strength
preconditions: User is on the 'Sign Up' screen

steps.content: Enter 'pass' into the password input field
steps.expected: Inline feedback (e.g., 'Password strength: Weak', 'Minimum 8 characters required') is displayed directly beneath the password field.

steps.content: Continue typing to 'Password123'
steps.expected: Inline feedback updates (e.g., 'Password strength: Medium', 'Needs special character').

steps.content: Continue typing to 'Password123!'
steps.expected: Inline feedback updates (e.g., 'Password strength: Strong'). All password requirement conditions are met.

testCases.expectedResult: Password field provides real-time inline feedback on password strength as the user types.

testCases.title: Contextual assistance for password requirements should disappear as conditions are met
preconditions: User is on the 'Sign Up' screen
Password requirements are displayed as contextual assistance (e.g., 'Min 8 characters', 'One uppercase', 'One number', 'One special character')

steps.content: Enter 'pass' into the password input field
steps.expected: Contextual assistance shows all password requirements as unmet.

steps.content: Continue typing to 'Password'
steps.expected: The 'Min 8 characters' and 'One uppercase' requirements are marked as met in the contextual assistance.

steps.content: Continue typing to 'Password1'
steps.expected: The 'One number' requirement is marked as met in the contextual assistance.

steps.content: Continue typing to 'Password1!'
steps.expected: The 'One special character' requirement is marked as met. All contextual assistance for password requirements disappears or indicates all conditions are met.

testCases.expectedResult: Contextual assistance for password requirements dynamically updates and disappears as each condition is met.

testCases.title: Primary action button should be disabled when required fields are empty on Sign In form
preconditions: User is on the 'Sign In' screen

steps.content: Ensure both email and password fields are empty
steps.expected: The 'Login' primary action button is disabled.

steps.content: Enter a valid email (e.g., 'test@example.com') but leave the password field empty
steps.expected: The 'Login' primary action button remains disabled.

steps.content: Clear the email field and enter a valid password (e.g., 'Password123!') into the password field
steps.expected: The 'Login' primary action button remains disabled.

testCases.expectedResult: The primary action button on the Sign In form remains disabled until both email and password fields contain input.

testCases.title: Alternative social login buttons should be clearly separated and display recognizable brand logos
preconditions: User is on the login/register screen

steps.content: Observe the area for alternative login methods
steps.expected: Social login buttons (e.g., Google, Facebook, Apple) are present and visually distinct from the primary email/password login form.

steps.content: Verify the design of each social login button
steps.expected: Each social login button displays a recognizable brand logo and is clearly separated from other login options.

testCases.expectedResult: Alternative social login methods are clearly presented with brand logos and separation.

testCases.title: Clicking a social login button should initiate the respective social authentication flow
preconditions: User is on the login/register screen
Social login buttons are visible (e.g., 'Login with Google')

steps.content: Click on one of the social login buttons (e.g., 'Login with Google')
steps.expected: The application redirects or opens a pop-up/native dialog for the selected social provider's authentication flow.

steps.content: Cancel the social login flow (if applicable)
steps.expected: User is returned to the login/register screen without being authenticated.

testCases.expectedResult: Social login buttons correctly initiate their respective authentication flows.

testCases.title: New Testcase for testing
preconditions: testing

steps.content: testing step
steps.expected: testing expect

testCases.title: Login/Register screen should load with minimalist, conversion-focused design on initial access
preconditions: User is not authenticated

steps.content: Navigate to the application's login/register entry point (e.g., launch app, click 'Login' button from homepage)
steps.expected: The login/register screen loads successfully. The UI appears minimalist and conversion-focused, with clear input fields for email and password, and a primary action button.

steps.content: Observe the overall layout and design elements
steps.expected: The design prioritizes a minimalist aesthetic. Primary action buttons are high-contrast. Alternative login methods are clearly separated.

testCases.expectedResult: The login/register screen is presented with a minimalist, conversion-focused design as per PRD.

testCases.title: Sign In and Sign Up states should transition seamlessly when user switches between them
preconditions: User is on the login/register screen

steps.content: Ensure the screen is in the 'Sign In' state (if not, navigate to it)
steps.expected: The 'Sign In' form is visible.

steps.content: Click on the 'Sign Up' option/tab
steps.expected: The screen transitions smoothly to the 'Sign Up' form with a noticeable animation. The user remains oriented during the transition.

steps.content: Click on the 'Sign In' option/tab
steps.expected: The screen transitions smoothly back to the 'Sign In' form with a noticeable animation. The user remains oriented during the transition.

testCases.expectedResult: Transitions between Sign In and Sign Up states are seamless and animated, keeping the user oriented.

testCases.title: Complex fields should be hidden until needed during the registration process
preconditions: User is on the 'Sign Up' screen

steps.content: Observe the initial 'Sign Up' form fields
steps.expected: Only essential fields (e.g., email, password) are immediately visible. Any 'complex' or optional fields (e.g., 'Confirm Password', 'Phone Number', 'Marketing Preferences') are hidden.

steps.content: Perform an action that would trigger the disclosure of a complex field (e.g., enter a valid email and password, or click a 'More Options' button if present)
steps.expected: The previously hidden complex field(s) (e.g., 'Confirm Password') become visible on the form.

testCases.expectedResult: The application utilizes progressive disclosure, showing complex fields only when needed, reducing initial cognitive load.
